title: Customer Portal, Bilingual UI, Services Redesign, Adaptive Gallery, and Full Admin Content Editor

requirements.summary: Implement customer self-service portal with email/mobile login, personal profile, and quotation history view
acceptanceCriteria: A customer can register using an email address or mobile number plus a password.
A customer can log in using their credentials and land on a personal portal page.
The portal page lists all work orders / quotations submitted by that customer with status, dates, and history.
Customers cannot see other customers' data.
Backend associates quotations with the customer's account by email or mobile number.

file_operations.description: Create a custom hook for customer authentication separate from Internet Identity. Implements registerCustomer and authenticateCustomer backend methods with password hashing (SHA-256). Manages customer session state using React Context or localStorage. Provides login, logout, register functions and current customer identifier state.

Create a React context provider for customer authentication state. Wraps the useCustomerAuth hook and exposes customer identity (email or mobile), authentication status, and auth functions to child components. Persists customer session across page reloads.

Create customer login page with form fields for email/mobile identifier and password. Uses the customer auth hook to call authenticateCustomer. On success, redirects to /customer/portal. Includes link to registration page. Supports bilingual labels (English and Telugu) using the LanguageContext.

Create customer registration page with form fields for email, mobile number, and password (with confirmation). Validates inputs and calls registerCustomer backend method via the customer auth hook. On success, redirects to login page. Supports bilingual labels.

Create customer portal page that fetches and displays all quotations for the logged-in customer using getCustomerQuotations backend method with the customer's identifier. Shows quotation ID, service type, status, timestamp, and negotiation history count. Includes a logout button. Requires customer authentication via a guard component.

Create a route guard component that checks if a customer is authenticated via CustomerAuthContext. If not authenticated, redirects to /customer/login. If authenticated, renders children. Similar pattern to AdminGuard but for customer auth flow.

Create a React Query hook that fetches quotations for a specific customer using getCustomerQuotations(customerId) backend method. Returns quotation list with status, dates, and history. Invalidates on quotation updates.
Add new routes for customer portal: /customer/login, /customer/register, and /customer/portal. Wrap the portal route with CustomerGuard. Ensure CustomerAuthContext provider wraps relevant routes.

Add a navigation link in the header or bottom nav to access the customer portal (/customer/login). Display customer name or identifier when a customer is logged in (from CustomerAuthContext). Ensure layout does not conflict with existing Internet Identity admin auth.
Wrap the App component with CustomerAuthProvider to ensure customer authentication state is available throughout the app. Place it inside QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.

requirements.summary: Display English and Telugu text side-by-side throughout the app without requiring a language toggle
acceptanceCriteria: All major headings and labels on every page show both English and Telugu text simultaneously.
No page requires switching language to see translated content.
Existing LanguageContext and translation files are leveraged without breaking the toggle for any remaining preference features.

file_operations.description: Create a utility function getBilingualText(enKey: string, teKey: string) that returns a formatted string combining English and Telugu translations side-by-side or stacked (e.g., 'English / తెలుగు'). Use this across all pages for headings, labels, and buttons.

Create a reusable component that renders English and Telugu text together. Accepts translationKey prop and displays both languages using a consistent style (e.g., English on top, Telugu below, or separated by a slash). Uses LanguageContext to fetch both translation sets.

Update all headings, labels, and button text to display both English and Telugu simultaneously using BilingualText component or getBilingualText utility. Replace single-language t() calls with bilingual rendering.
Update all service headings, descriptions, and category labels to show both English and Telugu text side-by-side. Use BilingualText component for consistent rendering.
Update hero section, benefit cards, and commitment section text to display bilingual content simultaneously. Apply BilingualText component to all translated strings.
Update all labels, headings, and button text on the contact page to display both English and Telugu. Use BilingualText for consistency.
Update category tabs, project titles, descriptions, and filter labels to show bilingual text. Ensure ProjectDetailModal also displays bilingual content.
Update page headings, filter labels, and rating text to display both English and Telugu simultaneously.
Update all form labels, placeholders, and button text to show bilingual content using BilingualText component.
Update navigation labels in header and BottomNav to display bilingual text for all menu items.
Update navigation label rendering to show both English and Telugu text for each tab item.

requirements.summary: Redesign services section to remove alphabetic labels, add sample images and videos, and enable admin management of service media
acceptanceCriteria: No alphabetic prefix labels (A, B, C, etc.) appear on any service listing.
Service cards display a sample image or video thumbnail relevant to the service type.
HomePage has a dedicated section showcasing printing sample images and embedded video previews.
Admin can upload/update service sample images and video URLs from the admin panel.

file_operations.description: Remove all alphabetic prefix labels (A, B, C) from service item rendering. Redesign service cards to display as visually attractive cards with sample images or video thumbnails fetched from backend using getServiceImages and getVideoClips methods. Use a grid layout with image/video preview, service title, and short description. Ensure bilingual text is displayed.

Add a new 'Printing Samples & Videos' section below the hero and features. Fetch service sample images using getServiceImages hook and video clips using getVideoClips hook. Display sample images from frontend/public/assets/generated/ (print-sample-banner.dim_800x450.png, print-sample-business-card.dim_800x450.png, print-sample-flex.dim_800x450.png, print-sample-brochure.dim_800x450.png) as static assets in a gallery grid. Display video thumbnails with play icons that link to video URLs. Use bilingual section headings.

Create React Query hooks to fetch, add, and delete service images using getServiceImages, addServiceImage, and deleteServiceImage backend methods. Returns array of ServiceImage objects with id, serviceType, imageUrl, and description.

Create React Query hooks to fetch, add, and delete video clips using getVideoClips, addVideoClip, and deleteVideoClip backend methods. Returns array of VideoClip objects with id, serviceType, videoUrl, thumbnailUrl, and description.

Create admin page for managing service sample images and video clips. Includes two sections: one for uploading/editing/deleting service images (with image preview and serviceType selector), and one for adding/editing/deleting video clips (with videoUrl and thumbnailUrl fields). Uses useServiceImages and useVideoClips hooks. Wrapped in AdminGuard. Supports bilingual labels.
Add route for /admin/service-media that renders ServiceMediaManagementPage wrapped in AdminGuard.
Add a quick-link card to navigate to /admin/service-media for managing service sample images and videos. Use bilingual label 'Service Media / సేవా మీడియా'.

Create a reusable ServiceCard component that accepts serviceType, imageUrl or videoThumbnailUrl, title (bilingual), and description (bilingual). Renders an attractive card with image/thumbnail, title overlay, and description. Used on ServicesPage and HomePage.

requirements.summary: Update ProjectGalleryPage to display images at natural aspect ratios using a masonry or variable-height grid layout
acceptanceCriteria: Gallery images are displayed with their original aspect ratios preserved (no forced crop to a single uniform height).
A masonry or variable-height grid layout is used so images of different shapes fit naturally.
The layout remains responsive across mobile and desktop screen sizes.
No image appears stretched or distorted.

file_operations.description: Replace the current uniform grid layout with a masonry or CSS columns layout that preserves each image's natural aspect ratio. Remove any fixed height or object-fit: cover styles that crop images. Use CSS columns (column-count, column-gap) or a masonry library (e.g., react-masonry-css) to create a variable-height grid. Ensure images load with their intrinsic width and height, scaled to fit the column width. Test with portrait, landscape, and square images. Maintain responsive breakpoints for mobile and desktop.

Create a reusable ProjectCard component that displays a project image, title, category badge, and description. The image should render at its natural aspect ratio without cropping. Accepts imageUrl, title, category, and description props. Used within the masonry layout on ProjectGalleryPage.

requirements.summary: Build comprehensive admin content editor for all dynamic content including contact details, services, business hours, gallery, and homepage/about content
acceptanceCriteria: Admin dashboard has management pages for: Contact Details, Services, Business Hours, Project Gallery, and Homepage/About content.
Admin can create, update, and delete entries in each of those sections.
All content changes are persisted to the backend and immediately reflected on the public-facing pages.
Business hours can be set per day (Mon–Sun) with open/close times.
No user-facing page contains hardcoded text that the admin should be able to change.
Only authenticated admins can access the edit interfaces.

file_operations.description: Create React Query hooks to fetch and update admin content using getAdminContent and updateAdminContent backend methods. AdminContent includes contactInfo, services array, businessHours array, gallery array, homepageContent, and aboutPageContent. Provides getAdminContent query and updateAdminContent mutation with query invalidation.

Create admin page for editing business hours. Displays a form with seven rows (Mon–Sun), each with open time and close time inputs (time pickers). Fetches current businessHours array from getAdminContent, allows editing, and saves via updateAdminContent mutation. Wrapped in AdminGuard. Supports bilingual labels.

Create admin page for editing homepage dynamic content (hero text, feature highlights, promotional banners). Displays a rich text editor or textarea for homepageContent field. Fetches from getAdminContent and saves via updateAdminContent. Wrapped in AdminGuard. Supports bilingual labels.

Create admin page for editing about page content. Displays a rich text editor or textarea for aboutPageContent field. Fetches from getAdminContent and saves via updateAdminContent. Wrapped in AdminGuard. Supports bilingual labels.

Replace hardcoded hero text, feature highlights, and promotional content with dynamic content fetched from getAdminContent hook (homepageContent field). Parse and render the content appropriately (e.g., as HTML or formatted text). Ensure bilingual display.
Replace hardcoded about page content with dynamic content fetched from getAdminContent hook (aboutPageContent field). Parse and render the content. Ensure bilingual display.
Update to display business hours fetched from getAdminContent hook (businessHours array). Render open/close times for each day of the week. Ensure bilingual day labels.
Add routes for /admin/business-hours, /admin/homepage-content, and /admin/about-content, each rendering the respective management page wrapped in AdminGuard.
Add quick-link cards to navigate to /admin/business-hours, /admin/homepage-content, and /admin/about-content for managing dynamic content. Use bilingual labels.

Ensure this page uses getAdminContent and updateAdminContent hooks instead of separate updateContactInfo mutation, so all admin content is managed via a unified interface. Update form to edit contactInfo field within AdminContent structure.

Ensure gallery management (add/edit/delete projects) uses the gallery array from AdminContent structure. Update to use getAdminContent and updateAdminContent for persisting gallery changes, in addition to existing useProjects hooks.
